data.epi_content:
"Why am I still alive instead of dead?"

That was the Crown Prince's first words as he grabbed my right hand that was tightly gripping the pistol.

He struggled to pry open my fingers one by one, trying to take the pistol. But as soon as my index finger straightened, the gun simply fell to the floor.

He bent down to pick up the pistol, but seemingly losing patience, he sighed once and then threw my right hand to the floor as well.

The handle was stained with blood that had once been red but was now turning brown as it dried.

"Typically, when a family falls from grace—and the greater the family, the more likely—most choose death, unable to accept the disparity between their former status and their current treatment by others. Whether it's murder or suicide, it hardly matters."

Yes, whether murder or suicide, I've died countless times.

But that's the problem—such simple solutions don't work anymore.

In this endlessly repeating time, watching people act the same or similarly makes even me feel stupid.

As if nothing ever happened, as if they've forgotten everything they did to me.

Who could possibly understand this feeling of despair and grief—not over what happened, but over the very fact that nothing happened at all?

Perhaps Evan might understand.

If he, like me, is going around and around in this world.

Like a foolish carp swimming in endless circles in a tiny pond, always seeing the same things.

"At the very least, I won't die.

Even if I die, I don't stay dead—what better example of divine immortality could there be?"

"Or have you gone mad and fallen into some cult?

That's not a particularly appealing option."

He picked up the pistol from the floor.

Then he tilted the magazine to check if there were bullets inside, pointed the muzzle at me, and cocked the hammer.

"Are you truly unable to die?

Can you survive even being shot?

Are you such a being, just as the omnipotent God?"

"I'm not sure about omnipotence."

At my words, he let out a derisive laugh.

After staring into my eyes for a long while, he struck my philtrum with the pistol grip, just as I had done to Lydia.

My mouth must have split open, as the scent and metallic taste of blood filled my mouth.

Perhaps out of disgust, I spat the red saliva that had pooled in my mouth onto the Crown Prince's face.

The reddened spit dripped stickily down his face.

He twitched an eyebrow once, then received a handkerchief and something wet that seemed to appear from nowhere, and wiped his face.

"Despair is fine, but if you're going to die, I'd prefer you do it alone in your room, so no one remembers—a lonely, miserable death."

I realized why I always end up dying.

And why no one helps me when I'm in trouble, yet they save others right before my eyes.

Why I can't cling to life or survive in this place.

Why, except for Evan and Vivian, no one likes me or approaches me with goodwill.

There should be at least one person, I would think.

"This isn't a suggestion. It's an order.

Wouldn't it be far better than having your head cut off, branded with the infamy of the Mecklenburg lady going mad and trying to kill the Crown Prince?"
data.epi_content2:
The monarch before me lacks dignity, but there's something about him—an aura surrounding him—as he wishes for my death.

A lonely, isolated, and therefore utterly miserable death.

"I wouldn't recommend being dragged to the execution grounds.

I'll assign an inexperienced executioner to handle you.

If it takes only two strikes, you'd be lucky—becoming the plaything of a clumsy young butcher who must strike again and again to sever your head. Would that be acceptable?"

It was excruciatingly painful.

So that's why it took only two strikes, if I was lucky.

"This is a place where everything I want, everything I wish for, comes true.

And am I not giving you an order disguised as a request? You should answer."

The Empire was, literally, the Emperor's country.

And the current Emperor is old, soon to be replaced by this young man before me.

Therefore, the Empire wishes for my death.

He came even closer to me, brushing the gun muzzle against my cheek several times.

With his left hand, he grabbed my chin and turned my face this way and that.

Then he smiled.

"Or, though I don't particularly recommend it, since you're somewhat pretty, I could give you the opportunity to do what any woman can do.

It's a personal preference of mine to keep educated, knowledgeable women with ambition or grudges in their hearts caged up, watching them wither away.

What do you think? You might not find happiness, but you could at least be a well-fed pig."

Every time this man breathed in front of me, blew tobacco smoke, spoke in that mocking tone, laughed at me, caressed my cheek, and looked at me as if I were worthless, I felt the tightly drawn strings in my mind beginning to snap one by one.

If only my hands were free, I would strangle him to death right now, and if only I had a gun, I wouldn't shoot his head or chest—I'd turn his limbs into a beehive first, then hack him to pieces with a shovel.

With such thoughts, I glared at the Crown Prince with murderous eyes, and he snorted and began beating my face mercilessly with the pistol grip.

Bound by shadows, I couldn't even fall backward, and took the blows directly to my face.

A blood vessel must have burst in my eye, as my right eye saw everything in red, and my head spun.

Judging by the wetness on my face, my nose was bleeding profusely.

I tried the only resistance I could manage.

I bit my tongue and spat blood at Johannes, this cursed man.

This time, something blocked the blood before it could reach his detestable face.

"You look at me as if I were your mortal enemy.

Ah, perhaps you know that I killed your family's eldest son worse than a dog?

The feeling of forcibly binding someone begging for their life and making their head fall—it was truly painful.

Look at these red tears of blood from my right eye. How else could I express such sorrow?"

Saying this, he wiped away the blood I had spat on his face and pressed his finger against me with force.

So forcefully that I thought my neck might snap if he applied just a little more pressure.

"Choose. I am so merciful that I give you options.

Either return to your room right now and blow your brains out, or become a flower in the imperial garden that I occasionally visit and pluck when I think of it."

When angry, my vision blurs and my breathing quickens.

But this was difficult to describe as anger or rage or any such emotion.

I just wanted to kill the man before me by any means necessary.

Even if I had to offer my soul to the devil.
data.epi_content3:
Even if I had to repeat my suffering many times over.

Then let me offer a sacrifice!

Like those crazy fanatics who rebelled in the territory, like those Blacks who eat people alive, biting and devouring them—let me offer a sacrifice!

What's stopping me? There are so many potential sacrifices right here.

With these thoughts, I took the pistol from the Crown Prince.

Then I sat down lightly on the bench.

I thought about changing the direction of the muzzle to shoot the Crown Prince, but as if reading my mind, he casually remarked:

"If you turn the gun on me, I'll turn you into a roly-poly doll and throw you to my cousin who's in puberty with raging hormones."

Come to think of it, how does one offer a sacrifice?

I don't know, I'll have to look into it next time.

Miss, this isn't suicide.

It's just being killed under coercion from another.

It's okay. I won't give up.

I lightly brushed the trigger with my finger, barely touching it, and before applying pressure, I looked at the Crown Prince and spoke calmly:

"Johannes. Your glorious, brilliant Empire will end with you.

Everyone will remember you as the historically incompetent ruler who drove the great Empire into a quagmire and ruined everything.

What can a man do who mercilessly kills capable people out of fear, using filthy excuses, who tramples the voices of lowly people begging for mercy while speaking of noble duties, who is so desperate to kill one woman before him who offends his eyes?"

He listened to my words silently, perhaps thinking I was reciting my last will.

When I stopped speaking, the Crown Prince asked with apparent boredom:

"Quite scathing. Are you finished?"

With that thought, I put the gun muzzle in my mouth and looked forward, noticing someone.

"Then pull that trigger right now."

Evan was watching me with a rigid face, tears streaming down, yet unable to move—as if someone had grabbed him tightly and frozen him in place.

He moved his lips.

Reading his lips, Evan was repeatedly saying "don't do it."

If you want to stop me so badly, you could run here right now and save me, why aren't you doing anything...

There might be some constraint on him.

Not my problem.

I put the gun muzzle in my mouth and tried to recall how I had pulled the trigger the first time.

Then I lowered the muzzle slightly and pulled the trigger... BANG!

With the gunshot, I collapsed to the floor with a mouthful of blood from the recoil.

But I didn't let go of the gun.

Ignoring all the bodily reactions and the physiological need to stop the bleeding immediately, I pulled the blood-soaked gun from my mouth.

He looked unusually startled.

I aimed at that worthless human and fired the gun with trembling hands.

While mockingly laughing at the coward.
